sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Atalanta have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, with a strong 3-0 win over Eintracht Frankfurt but suffering losses in domestic games like 0-3 to Sassuolo. Fiorentina, on the other hand, are in a dire run, winless in their last 7 matches, with their latest results including a 1-1 draw against Juventus and a 0-1 loss to AEK Athens. This suggests Atalanta hold a clear edge in momentum heading into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, results have been split, with Atalanta winning 3-2 at home in September 2024 and Fiorentina securing a 1-0 victory in March 2025. Matches between these sides often see goals from both teams, with 5 of their last 7 meetings featuring both teams scoring, indicating a competitive and open game could be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Atalanta, positioned 13th with 13 points, have a balanced attack and defense, scoring and conceding 14 goals each, while excelling in final third entries (ranked 4th in Serie A). Fiorentina, languishing in 19th with just 6 points, struggle defensively, conceding 19 goals, and have failed to secure a win this season, highlighting their vulnerability.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Atalanta’s Charles De Ketelaere, with 2 goals and a high expected assists tally (2.44), could be pivotal in unlocking Fiorentina’s defense. For Fiorentina, Rolando Mandragora, who overperformed his xG significantly (1.93) with 3 goals, remains a threat despite their poor form.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Atalanta’s strong ball possession (57%, 6th in Serie A) and ability to create chances in the final third could dominate Fiorentina, who often concede first (6/7 matches). Fiorentina’s tendency to pick up cards (5+ in their last 5 games) may lead to a physical battle, potentially disrupting their defensive structure.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Considering Atalanta’s superior form, home advantage, and Fiorentina’s ongoing winless streak, the Sofascore prediction of a 45% chance for a home win seems justified. Expect Atalanta to capitalize on Fiorentina’s defensive frailties, though the visitors might nick a goal given historical trends in head-to-heads.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: Atalanta leads Fiorentina 1-0 at halftime in this Serie A matchup at the New Balance Arena in Bergamo. The home side managed to edge ahead just before the break with a well-taken goal by Odilon Kossounou in the 41st minute. Atalanta has shown dominance in key areas, holding a slight edge in ball possession and creating more consistent pressure in the final third. Fiorentina, despite their efforts, struggled to convert their chances, missing a couple of significant opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Atalanta has been the more clinical side in the first half, capitalizing on their opportunities with a higher expected goals tally. Their frequent entries into the penalty area and ability to win duels, especially in the air, have given them a clear physical edge. Fiorentina, on the other hand, created a couple of big chances but failed to find the net, largely due to poor finishing. Their possession stats are close to Atalanta’s, but they’ve lacked the same level of threat in the attacking third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Atalanta, Charles De Ketelaere has been instrumental with his creativity, setting up plays and contributing significantly to their attacking moves. Odilon Kossounou, the goal scorer, has also been a standout with his composure in both defense and attack. On Fiorentina’s side, Roberto Piccoli showed intent with multiple attempts on goal but will need to be more clinical if they are to level the score. Their midfield will also need more from players like Simon Sohm to create better chances in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Atalanta starting with some early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. Fiorentina responded with a strong spell of attacking pressure shortly after, looking dangerous for a sustained period. However, Atalanta regained control with a prolonged phase of dominance in the middle of the half, consistently threatening Fiorentina’s defense. Towards the end of the half, momentum shifted back and forth, but Atalanta finished strongly, culminating in their goal just before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Fiorentina come out with renewed urgency as they chase an equalizer. Atalanta, buoyed by their lead, will aim to maintain their defensive solidity while looking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities. If Fiorentina can improve their finishing, they have the potential to turn the game around, but Atalanta’s confidence and home advantage could see them extend their lead. Expect both teams to push hard, with tactical adjustments likely to play a key role in deciding the outcome.
